macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Rallies to $79,000 on ETF Inflows and Short Squeeze
items.summary: Bitcoin surged 4.3% over 24 hours to reach $78,794.56, hitting a two-month high of $79,180 as $1.5 billion in spot Bitcoin ETF inflows provided support. The rally triggered approximately $200 million in short liquidations.

items.title: Altcoins Rally Alongside Bitcoin: Solana Up 2.7%, XRP Up 1.6%
items.summary: Major altcoins gained ground as Solana (SOL) traded at $87.37 with a 2.7% 24-hour increase, while XRP rose 1.6% to $1.44, reflecting broader market strength driven by institutional ETF activity.

items.title: Tron Founder Justin Sun Sues Trump-Linked World Liberty Financial Over WLFI Token Dispute
items.summary: Justin Sun filed a federal lawsuit in California against World Liberty Financial, alleging the development team maliciously blacklisted his wallet, stripped voting rights, and threatened to destroy his holdings. Sun had invested $75 million in WLFI tokens, making him the project's largest individual backer.

items.title: Crypto Market Shows Mixed Signals: ETF Support Offset by DeFi Stress
items.summary: Amberdata analysts identified four interconnected trends indicating the post-rally recovery driven by derivatives is ending, with ETF inflows providing spot support but perpetuals showing weakness, DeFi lending stressed, and stablecoin supply contracting.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT Image 2.5 with Major Photorealism Leap
items.summary: OpenAI's GPT Image 2.5 achieves the biggest jump in AI image generation in over a year, featuring perfect photorealistic human faces, advanced lighting, accurate complex text, flawless screenshots, and multiple images within images. It scores a +300 Elo jump on LLM Arena, enabling production-grade ads and fake dashboards from text prompts.[1]

items.title: Seedance 2.0 Enables AI-Driven Workflow from Blockout to Final Render
items.summary: Seedance 2.0 introduces a ComfyUI workflow that transforms raw CG playblasts into final rendered shots, preserving animation and camera while using AI for consistency and production-ready styles. This hybrid pipeline is expected to boost indie studios and reduce traditional rendering needs.[6]

items.title: AI Agent Operates Real San Francisco Storefront and Hires Humans
items.summary: An AI agent is running a physical storefront in San Francisco, managing operations and even hiring human employees, marking a practical advancement in AI autonomy for real-world business.[3][4]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Annapurna Announces Switch 2 Ports of Five Games
items.summary: Annapurna has announced Switch 2 ports of five games, with Sayonara Wild Hearts and Lorelei and the Laser Eyes releasing today. Owners of Switch versions can upgrade for free, with Stray coming next month.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Cuts Prices and Removes Launch-Day Call of Duty Games
items.summary: Xbox is cutting Game Pass prices, with Game Pass Ultimate dropping from $29.99 to $22.99 per month. However, new Call of Duty games will no longer launch day-one on the service.

items.title: Ecco the Dolphin Complete Collection Announced
items.summary: Ecco the Dolphin Complete has been announced, containing the first two games and a new 'contemporary' game, with development led by original creator Ed Annunziata.

items.title: Nintendo Sued Over Switch 2 Tariff Price Increases
items.summary: Players have sued Nintendo, claiming they should receive tariff refunds from the US government after Nintendo raised prices on some Switch 2 accessories due to Trump's widespread tariffs.

items.title: Koei Tecmo Revises Profit Forecast Upward by 50%
items.summary: Koei Tecmo has revised its FY2026 profit forecast 50% higher than originally predicted, citing strong game sales and investments.

items.title: Crimson Desert Version 1.04 Patch Released
items.summary: Crimson Desert's Version 1.04 patch is now rolling out on PC and consoles, adding difficulty levels, graphical improvements, and new storage items.

items.title: Google Exec: Almost Every Major Studio Uses AI in Game Development
items.summary: Google Cloud's games boss states that almost every major studio uses AI tools, though not all disclose it, citing Capcom as one major studio openly using AI.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: SpaceX Plans Substantial Capital Expenditures for In-House GPU Manufacturing
items.summary: SpaceX is planning major investments in manufacturing its own GPUs as part of its push into AI infrastructure ahead of a potential IPO, aiming to reduce reliance on external chip suppliers. This ties into Elon Musk's Terafab project involving SpaceX, Tesla, and xAI.

items.title: TSMC Delays Adoption of ASML's High-NA EUV Machines Until After 2029
items.summary: TSMC will hold off on using ASML's advanced high-NA EUV lithography machines, costing over €350M each, for chip production through 2029 to cut costs. The decision reflects efforts to manage expenses in semiconductor manufacturing.

items.title: Tesla to Use Intel's 14A Process for Chips at Terafab Project
items.summary: Elon Musk announced Tesla plans to utilize Intel's next-generation 14A manufacturing process for chips at its Terafab facility, positioning Tesla as the first major customer. This supports Tesla's expanded spending on robotics and infrastructure.

items.title: Tesla Boosts Capital Spending to $25 Billion for Robotics and Infrastructure
items.summary: Tesla has increased its capital expenditure plan to $25 billion, nearly triple the previous year's amount, focusing on robotics and related projects. The surge underscores aggressive expansion in hardware manufacturing.

items.title: Cloudsmith Raises $72M in Series C Funding
items.summary: Cloudsmith, a cloud-native platform for managing and securing software artifacts, secured $72 million in Series C funding after a $23M Series B in 2025. The investment supports growth in software supply chain security.
